Definition: Controls implemented using systems - Operating system controls
Administrative controls - Security policies
Controls implemented by people instead of systems - Security guards
Limit physical access - Guard shack
Block access to a resource - Examples: Firewall rules
Discourage an intrusion attempt - does not directly prevent access - Examples: Application splash screen
Identify and log an intrusion attempt - may not prevent access - Examples: System logs
Apply a control after event has been detected - reverse impact - continue operating with minimal downtime - Examples: Backup recovery
Control using other means - existing controls aren't sufficient
column3: may be temporary - Examples: Block instead of patch

Definition: Direct a subject towards security compliance - weak security control - Examples: File storage policies
Prevent disclosure of information to unauthorized individuals or systems - Uses encryption
Messages cannot be modified without detection - Uses hashing
Systems and networks must be up and running - Uses redundancy
Cannot deny what you've said - like a contract - Proof of integrity and origin - Digital signatures provide non-repudiation
Proving who you are - Username/password
What resources you can access - Permissions and access control lists
Tracking what users do - Logging
Compare current state vs. desired state - Identify security gaps - Create remediation plan - Baseline configuration
Never trust
column3: always verify - Assume breach mentality - Least privilege access - Micro-segmentation - Multi-factor authentication everywhere

Definition: Fences
column4: bollards - Security guards and reception - Badge readers and biometrics - Locks and access control vestibules - Video surveillance - Sensors and alarms

Public and private key pair - Public key encrypts

Definition: Longer keys = stronger encryption - Balance between security and performance
Encrypt entire drive - BitLocker
Encrypt specific partitions - Selective protection
Encrypt individual files - EFS (Encrypting File System) - GPG/PGP
Encrypt volumes/containers - VeraCrypt
Encrypt database fields - Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)
Encrypt specific records - Granular control
TLS/SSL for network traffic - VPNs (IPsec

Definition: One-way function - Fixed-length output - Verify data integrity - Algorithms: MD5 (weak)
Two inputs produce same hash - Security weakness
Add random data to input - Prevents rainbow table attacks - Unique salt per password
PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) - Bcrypt - Makes brute force attacks slower
Hash-based Message Authentication Code - Verify integrity and authenticity - Uses secret key with hash
Hash document - Encrypt hash with private key - Verify with public key - Provides: authenticity
Algorithm used for creating digital signatures
RSA algorithm used for digital signature creation and verification
Separate communication channel - Phone
Same communication channel - Diffie-Hellman
Key agreement protocol - Establish shared secret - DHE (Ephemeral)
Session keys not compromised if long-term key compromised - Uses ephemeral keys
Third party holds decryption keys - Recovery option - Privacy concerns
Make code difficult to understand - Protect intellectual property - Security through obscurity (not recommended as sole security)
Hide data within other data - Images
Replace sensitive data with token - Token has no meaning outside system - Common in payment processing
Hide original data - Show only necessary parts (e.g.
column3: last 4 digits of SSN) - Protects PII

Definition: Decentralized database - Multiple copies across network - No single point of control
